---
title: "Research"
toc: true
---

::: {.section-block .research-content}

My research focuses on quantitative defect spectroscopy and experimental data analysis for <span class="rs-mat">two-dimensional (2D) materials</span> and <span class="rs-mat">oxide semiconductors</span>. I aim to extract physically meaningful parameters from electrical and optical measurements and relate them directly to material properties and device behavior, with an emphasis on interpretability and reproducibility.

### 1. Defect & Optical Spectroscopy

- Quantitative analysis of intrinsic and extrinsic defects in <span class="rs-mat">2D materials</span> and <span class="rs-mat">oxide semiconductors</span>.
- Extraction of defect energy levels, densities, and carrier dynamics using <span class="rs-tech">DLTS</span> and <span class="rs-tech">PICTS</span>.
- <span class="rs-tech">Raman</span> / <span class="rs-tech">Photoluminescence (PL)</span> spectroscopy for probing defect states, doping, strain, and phase transitions.
- <span class="rs-tech">XPS</span> and <span class="rs-tech">spectroscopic ellipsometry (SE)</span> for chemical composition, bonding states, and optical properties.
- <span class="rs-tech">Tip-Enhanced Raman Spectroscopy (TERS)</span> for spatially resolved defect and structural inhomogeneity analysis beyond the diffraction limit.

### 2. Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) for Experimental Data

- Development of analysis pipelines for spectroscopic and electrical datasets, including preprocessing, feature extraction, modeling, and validation.
- <span class="rs-method">XAI</span> to identify physically relevant data features.
- Connection of <span class="rs-method">AI-extracted features</span> to defect-related mechanisms and material properties under data-limited experimental conditions.

### 3. Advanced Electronic & Photonic Devices

- Fabrication and characterization of electronic devices based on <span class="rs-mat">2D materials</span> and <span class="rs-mat">oxide semiconductors</span>, including logic and neuromorphic devices.
- Quantitative analysis of device electrical responses (e.g., I–V characteristics and transient behavior) to identify dominant transport and trapping mechanisms.
- <span class="rs-tech">FDTD</span> simulations to evaluate optical responses and electric-field distributions in nano- to micro-scale structures, supporting the design and analysis of photonic components.

:::
